cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A relatively permanent change in behavior or knowledge resulting from experience

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Learning in which a neutral stimulus becomes associated with an unconditioned stimulus to produce a conditioned response

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A stimulus that naturally triggers a response without prior learning

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The unlearned, automatic response to the unconditioned stimulus

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A previously neutral stimulus that, after pairing with the US, triggers a learned response

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The learned response to the conditioned stimulus

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The initial learning phase when an association between CS and US is established

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The weakening of a conditioned response when the CS is presented without the US

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The reappearance of an extinguished conditioned response after a rest period

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Responding to stimuli similar to the conditioned stimulus

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The learned ability to distinguish between the CS and similar stimuli

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Learning in which behavior is shaped by its consequences — reinforcement or punishment

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Adding a pleasant stimulus after a behavior to increase that behavior

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Removing an unpleasant stimulus after a behavior to increase that behavior

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Adding an unpleasant stimulus after a behavior to decrease that behavior

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Removing a pleasant stimulus after a behavior to decrease that behavior

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Reinforcing successive approximations toward a target behavior

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Reinforcement delivered after a set number of responses

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Reinforcement delivered after an unpredictable number of responses; produces highest response rate

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Reinforcement delivered for the first response after a set time period

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Reinforcement delivered for the first response after an unpredictable time period

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A reinforcer that is inherently satisfying and does not require learning to be effective (e.g., food, water)

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A reinforcer that gains its value through association with a primary reinforcer (e.g., money, grades)

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Learning that occurs without obvious reinforcement and is not demonstrated until an incentive is provided

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Learning by watching and imitating models without direct reinforcement

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A learned avoidance of a specific food after it was paired with nausea, even with a long delay

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The passive resignation that occurs when an organism learns it cannot control aversive events

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A chamber with a lever or key that an animal presses to obtain reinforcement, used in operant conditioning research

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A procedure in which the CS is presented before the US and overlaps with it — the most effective timing for classical conditioning

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Higher-order conditioning in which a CS is paired with another CS to produce a new conditioned response

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Ivan Pavlov
cards.answer: Discovered classical conditioning through experiments on salivation in dogs

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: B.F. Skinner
cards.answer: Developed operant conditioning theory and the Skinner box for studying reinforcement

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Albert Bandura
cards.answer: Demonstrated observational learning through the Bobo doll experiment

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Edward Thorndike
cards.answer: Proposed the law of effect — behaviors followed by satisfying consequences are more likely to be repeated

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: John B. Watson
cards.answer: Demonstrated classical conditioning of fear in the Little Albert experiment

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Edward Tolman
cards.answer: Demonstrated latent learning and cognitive maps in rats navigating mazes

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: John Garcia
cards.answer: Discovered taste aversion learning, showing biological constraints on classical conditioning

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Wolfgang Köhler
cards.answer: Demonstrated insight learning in chimpanzees solving problems without trial and error

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Martin Seligman
cards.answer: Discovered learned helplessness in dogs, later applied to understanding depression

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A child watches an older sibling get praised for cleaning their room, so the child also starts cleaning — example of what type of learning?

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A dog salivates when it hears its owner open the treat bag — the sound is the:

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Slot machines deliver payoff after an unpredictable number of pulls — example of what schedule?

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Taking aspirin to relieve a headache — the headache removal increases aspirin-taking behavior. This is:

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A child who is bitten by a golden retriever becomes afraid of all dogs — this demonstrates:

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Thorndike's principle that behaviors followed by satisfying consequences tend to be repeated

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The biological readiness to learn certain associations more easily than others

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A sudden realization of a problem's solution without trial-and-error

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Reinforcement of every correct response

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Reinforcing responses only some of the time; produces behavior more resistant to extinction

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The principle that behavior learned on a partial schedule is harder to extinguish than continuously reinforced behavior

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Mental representations of a physical space, used to navigate without visible reinforcement

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A type of reinforcer that uses tokens or points exchangeable for primary reinforcers

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A teenager loses phone privileges for breaking curfew — this is an example of:

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Using an existing conditioned stimulus to condition a new neutral stimulus

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Bandura's four steps for observational learning: attention, retention, reproduction, and motivation

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Brain cells that fire both when performing an action and when observing the same action performed by others

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A previously conditioned response is triggered by a new stimulus similar to the original CS, but without explicit new conditioning

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A factory worker is paid $5 for every 20 widgets assembled — example of what reinforcement schedule?

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Checking your email randomly throughout the day because messages arrive unpredictably — example of what schedule?

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: An innate behavior pattern that disrupts operant conditioning because the animal reverts to natural tendencies
